runtime.settings_state: outside-Warp local control and all granular outside-Warp categories preloaded true in private local preferences; inside-Warp authenticated-user actions false
runtime.api_key_denial_note: A dummy WARPCTRL_API_KEY value was used for the API-key denial case. No real API key was printed or committed.

commands.context: outside_warp external xterm with dummy API-key-like env var
commands.permission_state: Outside-Warp local control was enabled with all granular outside-Warp categories preloaded in private local preferences for the isolated profile. The app was still first-run/logged-out, inside-Warp authenticated-user actions were disabled, and no verified Warp-terminal proof/API-key exchange implementation was available. A dummy WARPCTRL_API_KEY was supplied only for the workflow-list denial case and was not logged as a secret.
commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: The terminal should show the exact JSON-mode drive list command and a structured denial, while the Warp UI remains logged out/onboarding with no Drive data exposed.
commands.proof_setup_notes: Use a staggered outside terminal plus the visible logged-out Warp app. Include a dummy API-key-like env var to prove the external/API-key path does not grant authenticated Drive access.
commands.expected_result: Structured JSON denial with execution_context_not_allowed before Drive data is exposed.
commands.required_permission_category: read_metadata plus authenticated_user requirement for Drive

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: A JSON error should be visible in the external terminal and the Warp app should remain on logged-out/onboarding with no Drive objects shown.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: Screenshot shows a staggered external xterm on the left with the command/output and the Warp app on the right. The Warp app remains on the first-run/logged-out Welcome to Warp screen; no Drive data, object content, environment-variable data, terminal command execution, or output containing warpctrl-validation appears in the Warp UI.
visual_inspection.unexpected_ui_changes: None observed. Warp remained on onboarding/logged-out state.
visual_inspection.ambiguous_or_blocking_artifacts: The Warp UI is partially covered by the xterm in the staggered composition, but the Welcome to Warp screen and lack of target-side mutation remain visible.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: First obtain a Drive object ID from a successful authenticated drive list response, then show the inspect JSON output without exposing secrets.
commands.proof_setup_notes: No Drive object ID was available because all Drive list commands correctly failed closed from outside-Warp with execution_context_not_allowed.
commands.expected_result: Skipped; requires ID from successful drive list and authenticated-user grant.
commands.required_permission_category: read_underlying_data plus authenticated_user requirement for Drive inspect

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Would show JSON inspect result for a disposable Drive object only after an authenticated list success supplied an ID.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: No command screenshot because the command was intentionally not executed without an ID.
visual_inspection.ambiguous_or_blocking_artifacts: Blocked by lack of authenticated Drive list success / no Drive object ID.

commands.skip_or_blocker_reason: Drive list failed closed before returning any DRIVE_OBJECT_ID_FROM_DRIVE_LIST; executing inspect with a fabricated ID would not validate the requested command semantics.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Create/select a disposable workflow, show its ID from authenticated drive list, then run it and show the terminal/UI/audit result without exposing user data.
commands.proof_setup_notes: No workflow ID was available because workflow listing correctly failed closed from outside-Warp with execution_context_not_allowed.
commands.expected_result: Skipped; requires workflow ID from successful drive list and authenticated execution/Drive workflow grant.
commands.required_permission_category: mutate_underlying_data plus authenticated_user requirement for workflow execution

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: Would show a workflow execution result for a disposable workflow only after authenticated Drive access supplied an ID.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: No command screenshot because the command was intentionally not executed without a workflow ID.
visual_inspection.ambiguous_or_blocking_artifacts: Blocked by lack of authenticated workflow list success / no WORKFLOW_ID_FROM_DRIVE_LIST.

commands.skip_or_blocker_reason: Workflow list failed closed before returning any WORKFLOW_ID_FROM_DRIVE_LIST; executing workflow run with a fabricated ID would not validate the requested command semantics.

blockers: Authenticated Drive inspect could not be visually validated because drive list correctly failed closed and returned no object ID.
Authenticated workflow run could not be visually validated because workflow list correctly failed closed and returned no workflow ID.
No verified Warp-terminal proof was available in this foundation build/session, and the app remained first-run/logged-out despite launch with a staging API key.

The implemented catalog marks Drive and input.run as authenticated-user actions allowed only from inside_warp; outside-Warp/API-key scripting is not implemented in this checked-out commit, so external attempts fail before target resolution.
The GUI launched into first-run/logged-out onboarding; no authenticated Drive objects or verified terminal proof could be established for success-path validation.
Drive inspect and workflow run require IDs from successful authenticated drive list output; no IDs were available after fail-closed list denials.
